mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for equity valuations and lower corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending earnings recession, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or threshold crossing fails to predict direction with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in new house sales and housing starts confirms a broad housing cycle upswing that amplifies consumer spending and financial sector revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC > 0.05, or housing data diverges from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO momentum over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers Federal Reserve rate hike expectations that compress equity multiples and increase discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or flat during inflation acceleration phases, or the inflation-rate interaction term loses predictive power (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment gains exceeding 150 thousand persons confirm robust labor demand that sustains consumer spending growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll surges above 150k fail to predict positive 60d returns, or the signal produces negative returns in > 55% of triggered months over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth outpacing the unemployment rate decline indicates a non-inflationary economic expansion that supports corporate earnings without triggering monetary tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDP-unemployment interaction term yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive GDP growth coincides with negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth lagging behind consumer price inflation signals capacity constraints and input cost pressures that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive during supply-demand imbalances, or the lagging production signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).
